column1:
Outbound only. Your store does NOT accept inbound connections from ironcart.dev. You control when scans run by editing the schedule below.
Off by default. Toggling this to Yes enables the ironcart_scan_upload_cron job, which runs bin/magento ironcart:scan --upload on the schedule below and logs the result to var/log/ironcart_scan.log.
column2:

column1: After the scan completes, upload the report to ironcart.dev for hosted viewing. Requires Stores → Configuration → Ironcart → Scan Upload → Enable, plus a token. Off by default. See docs/UPLOAD.md.
column2: After the scan completes, upload the report to ironcart.dev for hosted viewing. Requires Stores → Configuration → Ironcart → Scan Upload → Enable, plus a token. Off by default. See docs/UPLOAD.md.

column1: Bearer token from ironcart.dev. Stored encrypted via Magento's standard encryption backend.
column2: Bearer token from ironcart.dev. Stored encrypted via Magento's standard encryption backend.

column1: Hard host-pin for the upload client. Leave blank to use the production value (ironcart.dev). Defense in depth — even if the Endpoint URL is overwritten at runtime, the upload client will refuse to POST anywhere whose host does not match this exact string.
column2: Hard host-pin for the upload client. Leave blank to use the production value (ironcart.dev). Defense in depth — even if the Endpoint URL is overwritten at runtime, the upload client will refuse to POST anywhere whose host does not match this exact string.

column1: Include admin usernames in finding evidence. Off by default — usernames are PII under the IronCartM2 v0 policy and must be explicitly opted into per-run.
column2: Include admin usernames in finding evidence. Off by default — usernames are PII under the IronCartM2 v0 policy and must be explicitly opted into per-run.

column1: Leave blank to use the production endpoint (https://ironcart.dev/api/scan/ingest). Override only for QA against a local Next.js dev server. The destination host must match the Allowed Host field below or the upload client will reject the URL before any socket is opened.
column2: Leave blank to use the production endpoint (https://ironcart.dev/api/scan/ingest). Override only for QA against a local Next.js dev server. The destination host must match the Allowed Host field below or the upload client will reject the URL before any socket is opened.

column1: Off by default. Toggling this to Yes enables `bin/magento ironcart:scan --upload`.
column2: Off by default. Toggling this to Yes enables `bin/magento ironcart:scan --upload`.

column1:
Optional: paste an Ironcart Pro license blob to flag uploads from this store as pro-tier. All 43 checks always run regardless of license state — this group only controls how ironcart.dev tags the uploaded scan run.

The blob is verified offline against a compiled-in Ed25519 public key; no outbound network call leaves your store as part of this verification. Get a blob by subscribing at ironcart.dev/pro and pasting the value from your account dashboard.
column2:
Optional: paste an Ironcart Pro license blob to flag uploads from this store as pro-tier. All 43 checks always run regardless of license state — this group only controls how ironcart.dev tags the uploaded scan run.

The blob is verified offline against a compiled-in Ed25519 public key; no outbound network call leaves your store as part of this verification. Get a blob by subscribing at ironcart.dev/pro and pasting the value from your account dashboard.

column1:
Optional: run bin/magento ironcart:scan --upload on a Magento cron schedule so ironcart.dev always has a fresh view of your store's security posture. Off by default.

Outbound only. Your store does NOT accept inbound connections from ironcart.dev. You control when scans run by editing this schedule.

Requires the Scan Upload group above to be enabled and a token pasted — the cron reuses the same token (no separate credential surface).

Documentation: ironcart.dev/docs/scanner/continuous-monitoring.
column2:

Requires the Scan Upload group above to be enabled and a token pasted — the cron reuses the same token (no separate credential surface).

Documentation: ironcart.dev/docs/scanner/continuous-monitoring.

column1:
Optional: upload `bin/magento ironcart:scan` results to ironcart.dev for hosted viewing, alerting, and team sharing. Off by default.

What gets sent: scan findings, composer package list, Magento version + edition, and the store base URL.
What is never sent: your Magento admin email, customer or order PII, secrets, or any cookies/sessions from this server.

To get a token: sign up at ironcart.dev/scanner (or claim an existing anonymous scan) and copy the token into the field below.

Full wire-contract documentation: ironcart.dev/docs/scanner/upload.
column2:
Optional: upload `bin/magento ironcart:scan` results to ironcart.dev for hosted viewing, alerting, and team sharing. Off by default.

What gets sent: scan findings, composer package list, Magento version + edition, and the store base URL.
What is never sent: your Magento admin email, customer or order PII, secrets, or any cookies/sessions from this server.

To get a token: sign up at ironcart.dev/scanner (or claim an existing anonymous scan) and copy the token into the field below.

Full wire-contract documentation: ironcart.dev/docs/scanner/upload.

column1: Paste the full base64url-encoded blob from your ironcart.dev account. Stored encrypted via Magento's standard encryption backend. Leave blank to run as free-tier (default). Rotating: paste a new blob to replace the existing one; there is no separate revocation step.
column2: Paste the full base64url-encoded blob from your ironcart.dev account. Stored encrypted via Magento's standard encryption backend. Leave blank to run as free-tier (default). Rotating: paste a new blob to replace the existing one; there is no separate revocation step.

column1: Run the Ironcart read-only Magento security scan.
column2: Run the Ironcart read-only Magento security scan.

column1: Sends package name + version only. No domain, admin user, IP, or store identifier. The destination host is pinned to ironcart.dev.
column2: Sends package name + version only. No domain, admin user, IP, or store identifier. The destination host is pinned to ironcart.dev.

column1:
Standard crontab string consumed by Magento's cron framework. Default: 0 3 * * * (daily at 03:00 store-server time). Examples:
0 */6 * * * — every 6 hours.
0 2 * * 1 — Mondays at 02:00.
Magento re-reads this value at the start of each cron tick; no cron:install reboot is required after a change. The schedule only fires when Enable scheduled scan + upload above is set to Yes.
column2:

column1: When enabled, the IC-060 check POSTs the installed Composer package list (name + version, no PII) to https://ironcart.dev/api/cve for OSV.dev cross-referencing. Disabled by default to preserve the module's read-only, no-outbound-network invariant.
